features.title: Define system architecture and bounded contexts
features.description: Finalize the initial service boundaries, domain model, and V1 architecture for auth, billing, model gateway, asset library, and generation flows.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture doc identifies major services and responsibilities
Core entities and relationships are documented
Primary V1 assumptions and out-of-scope items are listed

session_notes.note: Created the initial architecture definition, bounded contexts, service layout, request flow, and phased V1 roadmap in docs/architecture.md and docs/v1-roadmap.md.

features.title: Implement identity, tenant, project, and RBAC model
features.description: Design and implement the core authorization model for super admins, tenant owners, project admins, and users.
acceptance_criteria: Users can belong to tenants and projects
Roles support platform-level and tenant/project-level permissions
Permission matrix is documented and testable

session_notes.note: Starting implementation of identity, tenant, project, and RBAC domain model.
Implemented the core identity and RBAC domain model in platform_core/identity.py, including users, tenants, projects, memberships, built-in roles, permission evaluation, and a documented RBAC matrix.

features.title: Design unified model provider registry and capability schema
features.description: Create the Python-side abstraction for providers, models, capabilities, status, and vendor replacement within the platform.
acceptance_criteria: Model registry supports multiple providers and multiple modalities
Capability types cover text, image, and video generation
Registry can map platform model aliases to vendor-specific identifiers

session_notes.note: Starting implementation of model provider registry and capability schema.

Implemented a unified model provider registry in platform_core/models.py with provider definitions, platform model aliases, capability types, metering dimensions, provider bindings, route resolution, and default seeded mappings for copy, image, and video generation.

features.title: Implement platform pricing, metering units, and ledger model
features.description: Define the Java-owned internal price units and billing records so vendor changes do not break tenant billing.
acceptance_criteria: Pricing model supports token, image, and video-second dimensions
Ledger records both internal price and underlying vendor reference
Historical charges remain stable after future price changes

session_notes.note: Starting implementation of pricing, metering units, and ledger model.

Implemented the billing core in platform_core/billing.py with platform pricing rules, metered usage inputs, charge quoting, vendor reference snapshots, immutable ledger entries, recharge handling, and seeded pricing for copy, image, and video capabilities.

features.title: Implement tenant wallet recharge and quota allocation
features.description: Implement Java-side tenant wallet management and quota allocation for projects and users.
acceptance_criteria: Tenant wallet supports recharge records and balance queries
Quota rules can be set per user and per project
Quota dimensions include tokens, images, video seconds, and daily limits

session_notes.note: Starting Java-side implementation of tenant wallet recharge and quota allocation.

Implemented a Java Maven module for tenant finance with wallet ledger entries, recharge and usage deduction services, quota allocations for project and user scopes, and quota consumption checks across token, image, video, daily-request, and concurrency dimensions.

features.title: Implement super-admin model access control
features.description: Allow super admins through Java admin APIs to control which tenants, roles, or projects can use which platform models.
acceptance_criteria: Super admin can enable or disable models by scope
Model access policy is enforced before every generation request
Policy decisions are auditable

session_notes.note: Starting Java-side implementation of super-admin model access control.
Implemented Java-side model access control with scoped allow/deny rules for tenants, projects, and roles, plus deterministic policy evaluation and audit events for rule creation and disable actions.

features.title: Implement brand, client, and asset library domain
features.description: Support advertiser clients, their brands, and brand-scoped assets and rules inside each tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Tenant can manage clients and brands
Assets can be tagged to tenant, project, client, and brand
Brand rules support style guidance and forbidden statements

session_notes.note: Starting Java-side implementation of brand, client, and asset library domain.
Implemented the Java asset-library domain with clients, brands, brand rules, assets, brand profile aggregation, and asset filtering by tenant, project, client, brand, kind, and tags.

features.title: Implement grounded prompt assembly for brand-safe generation
features.description: Implement Python-side prompt assembly from user input, project context, brand rules, and selected assets so output follows brand constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Generation requests can reference brand and selected assets
Prompt assembly injects brand guidance and forbidden terms
Prompt context generation is traceable for audit

session_notes.note: Starting Python-side grounded prompt assembly for brand-safe generation.
Implemented Python-side grounded prompt assembly with brand context, structured brand rules, referenced assets, generation intent, and audit metadata for traceable prompt construction.

features.title: Implement generation job orchestration for copy, image, and video
features.description: Build the Python-side task flow for multiple AIGC modalities with sync and async execution support, integrated with Java business services.
acceptance_criteria: Copy generation works through the model gateway
Image generation works through the model gateway
Video generation supports asynchronous task tracking

session_notes.note: Starting Python-side generation job orchestration for copy, image, and video.

Implemented Python-side generation orchestration with model-route resolution, grounded prompt assembly, provider invocation payload construction, synchronous copy and image dispatch, and asynchronous video job lifecycle handling.

features.title: Implement usage deduction, settlement, and quota enforcement
features.description: Tie model usage to internal prices, wallet deduction, and quota checks across users and projects.
acceptance_criteria: Every generation task produces meterable usage records
Charges deduct from tenant wallet using internal pricing
Quota violations block requests with clear errors

session_notes.note: Starting usage settlement and quota enforcement flow.
Implemented Python-side settlement coordination that maps generation results into metered usage, enforces project and user quotas, applies internal pricing, and records tenant ledger deductions.

features.title: Implement audit logs, safety policies, and admin observability
features.description: Provide auditability for admin actions and generation tasks, plus safety and compliance hooks.
acceptance_criteria: Critical admin actions are logged
Generation tasks keep request, result summary, and cost records
Safety policy violations are captured and queryable

session_notes.note: Starting audit, safety policy, and observability implementation.

Implemented Java-side admin observability with admin audit events, safety policies, and safety incidents, plus Python-side generation audit records and forbidden-term safety evaluation for model outputs.

features.title: Define V1 delivery slices and implementation roadmap
features.description: Translate the feature backlog into phased milestones so the build can proceed incrementally.
acceptance_criteria: Backlog is grouped into release slices
Each slice has clear dependencies
The first implementation slice is actionable by a coding agent

session_notes.note: Starting V1 delivery slicing and implementation roadmap finalization.

Expanded the V1 roadmap into a concrete delivery plan with milestone slices, demo scenarios, release recommendations, and an explicit next-phase shift from domain modeling to Spring Boot APIs and a first visible admin UI.

features.title: Replace mock WeChat recharge with real payment integration
features.description: The current tenant recharge flow uses a mock WeChat payment order and simulated success callback. Replace it with real WeChat Pay configuration, order signing, callback verification, and production-grade payment state handling.
acceptance_criteria: Payment configuration can be injected from application config or environment variables
WeChat order creation uses real signed requests instead of mock QR payloads
Asynchronous callback verification updates order status and wallet recharge idempotently

session_notes.note: Current recharge flow is implemented with mock WeChat payment orders and a simulated payment-success action. Replace this with real WeChat Pay parameters, callback verification, and idempotent payment settlement in a later delivery slice.

features.title: Split Java backend into Spring Cloud Alibaba microservices
features.description: Replace the current single Spring Boot backend with Spring Cloud Alibaba services, Nacos service discovery, and clear platform-versus-tenant service boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Platform management service and tenant workspace service run as separate Spring Boot applications
Both services use Spring Cloud Alibaba Nacos discovery configuration
Database split strategy is documented as same PostgreSQL server with different databases

session_notes.note: Started migrating the Java backend from a single Spring Boot application to a Spring Cloud Alibaba microservice layout. Platform management stays in chj-aigc-platform-service first, while a dedicated backend-tenant-service is introduced and both services are prepared for Nacos registration and discovery.
Added backend-gateway-service as the unified Spring Cloud Gateway entry service and prepared explicit platform and tenant route prefixes so the frontend can later switch to a single gateway entry.

Switched the frontend development proxy to the gateway service. The gateway now exposes `/api/**` as the unified frontend entry and forwards it to the platform service, while `/tenant-api/**` remains available for the next migration batch.

features.title: Migrate tenant workspace APIs from platform service to tenant service
features.description: Move tenant-side project, member, asset, wallet, and quota APIs into the dedicated tenant workspace microservice and then place an API gateway in front of both Java services.
acceptance_criteria: Tenant workspace APIs are no longer served by the platform management service
Frontend can call tenant APIs through a single routed entry after gateway introduction
Service-to-service contracts between platform and tenant services are documented

session_notes.note: The current tenant APIs still live in backend-java for continuity. They need to be moved into backend-tenant-service in a follow-up delivery slice, with a gateway added afterward.

Migrated tenant login, project, member, and quota APIs into backend-tenant-service with MyBatis XML persistence, compatibility handling for the shared PostgreSQL schema, and gateway routing for `/api/auth/**` plus `/api/tenant/**`.

Migrated tenant client, brand, and asset APIs into backend-tenant-service, added XML mappers plus Chinese-commented schema compatibility for shared PostgreSQL tables, and added a local Nacos docker compose setup for Spring Cloud Alibaba development.
Extended backend-tenant-service with tenant wallet snapshot and mock WeChat payment order APIs, and added non-Docker local Nacos download/start/stop PowerShell scripts for Windows development.

Added a discovery profile for backend-gateway-service with lb:// routes and added local PowerShell scripts to start platform service, tenant service, and gateway service through Nacos registration and discovery.

Removed `/api/auth/**` and `/api/tenant/**` controllers from chj-aigc-platform-service so platform service no longer serves tenant workspace or login APIs directly. The unified frontend entry now depends on gateway routing those paths to backend-tenant-service.

Prepared PostgreSQL split-database configuration by separating platform schema ownership, adding create-microservice-databases.sql, and documenting the remaining auth-table dependency that still blocks a full physical split.
Added `X-Trace-Id` propagation and request tracing logs for gateway, platform service, and tenant service. Future services must follow the same trace-header and logging convention.

features.title: Containerize all application services except PostgreSQL
features.description: Add Dockerfiles and unified local deployment definitions for the Java platform service, Java tenant service, Java gateway service, Python model service, frontend admin service, and Nacos, while keeping PostgreSQL external.
acceptance_criteria: Each application service has a production-usable Dockerfile
A unified local compose definition can start all services except PostgreSQL
Environment variables and inter-service addresses are documented for container deployment

session_notes.note: User requires all services except PostgreSQL to support container deployment later. Add Dockerfiles and a unified compose stack after the core microservice split is stable.

features.title: Split identity storage away from platform direct database reads
features.description: Remove the remaining direct dependency on auth_users and auth_sessions across platform and tenant services so PostgreSQL can be physically split into chj-aigc-platform and chj-aigc-tenant without shared tables.
acceptance_criteria: Platform service no longer queries tenant-owned auth tables directly
Tenant member and tenant summary data can be obtained through a service contract or a dedicated identity service
Platform and tenant services can run on separate PostgreSQL databases without duplicated auth writes

session_notes.note: Physical PostgreSQL split is not complete yet because both platform and tenant flows still depend on auth_users and auth_sessions. Add a dedicated identity ownership step before switching both services to separate databases.
Renamed backend-java to chj-aigc-platform-service and started a dedicated auth-service split so platform and tenant services can stop sharing direct auth table ownership.

features.title: Standardize trace logging for all future services
features.description: Ensure every new service, including Python model services and future Java microservices, participates in the same request trace convention based on X-Trace-Id propagation and structured request logs.
acceptance_criteria: Every new service accepts and propagates X-Trace-Id
Every new service writes request logs with traceId and duration
Cross-service troubleshooting can correlate gateway, business service, and model-service logs by traceId

session_notes.note: User requires all later services to keep the same trace logging convention. Extend X-Trace-Id propagation and request-duration logging to Python model services and any future Java services.
在 platform_core/trace.py 中实现 Python 侧链路追踪工具，提供 TraceLogger、trace_request 装饰器和 traceId 上下文传播，与 Java 微服务 X-Trace-Id 约定保持一致。

features.title: Start Python model gateway service and validate generation API
features.description: backend-model-service (port 8084) has a FastAPI skeleton but has never been started. Install dependencies, start the service, validate the health endpoint, and wire the generation router into the Java platform service call chain. Also expand platform_core/models.py to register all confirmed provider models.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/model/health returns 200
POST /api/model/jobs accepts a generation request and returns a job result
All confirmed provider models are registered in platform_core/models.py
Service startup is added to start-all.cmd

session_notes.note: Starting feature-019. Confirmed model list from user: 文生图: 阿里 Qwen-Image-2.0, 谷歌 Gemini 3.0 Pro Image; 文生视频: 阿里 Wan2.6-T2V, 字节 Seedance 2.0, OpenAI Sora 2; 图生视频: 阿里 Wan2.6-I2V, 快手 可灵 2.0/O1, 北大&字节 Helios; 文生动漫: Midjourney Niji 7, 阿里 Wan2.6; 图生动漫: 阿里 Wan2.6-I2V, 快手 可灵 O1; 混合制作: Qwen-Image-2.0+Wan2.6-I2V, DALL-E 3+Sora 2; AI搜索: Perplexity; 深度研究: ChatGPT深度研究; 全能助手: ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude.

Completed feature-019. Expanded CapabilityType with IMAGE_TO_VIDEO, ANIME_GENERATION, IMAGE_TO_ANIME, HYBRID_PRODUCTION, AI_SEARCH, DEEP_RESEARCH. Registered 8 providers and 20+ platform models in seed_default_registry(). Created backend-model-service/src/provider_config.py for API key configuration. Fixed generation.py router to use dispatch() and correct BrandContext/GenerationIntent fields. Service starts on port 8084, health and jobs endpoints validated. Added to start-all.cmd.

features.title: Implement end-to-end AIGC generation core pipeline
features.description: Wire the full generation flow: brand-safe prompt assembly, model access policy check, provider adapter invocation, usage metering, wallet deduction, and result persistence. Implement at least one real provider adapter (e.g. OpenAI for copy, image).
acceptance_criteria: Copy generation request flows through brand grounding, model gateway, and returns result
Image generation request flows through brand grounding, model gateway, and returns result
Video generation submits async job and status can be polled
Wallet is deducted and quota is consumed after each successful generation

session_notes.note: Implemented the Java-to-Python generation chain through backend-tenant-service: model access decision, provider invocation, generation persistence, quota consumption, wallet settlement, and frontend workbench wiring for copy/image/video requests.

Local validation now uses infra/dev/README.md as the canonical startup guide. Verified local ports 8848/8080/8081/8082/8084/5173 are responding. Deleted misleading root startup scripts (start-all.cmd, start-all-windows.bat, start-nacos.bat, start-all.vbs, start-missing.vbs) so later agents do not bypass the documented infra/dev PowerShell launch flow. Remaining work: finish browser-side tenant generation verification and capture any final integration fixes.

补充启动注意事项：tenant-service 的标准本地启动入口是 infra/dev/start-tenant-service.ps1；如果需要可视化 cmd 窗口，应该由 cmd 去调用这个 PowerShell 脚本，而不是重新手写一整套环境变量。tenant-service 冷启动或重新编译可能超过 3 分钟，必须同时检查 8082 health 和 Nacos 实例列表，再判断是否真正启动失败。

完成本地联调修复与浏览器验证：1) infra/dev 启动脚本增加 JDK21/Maven 兜底，避免系统遗留 JAVA_HOME=JDK8 导致 spring-boot:run 失败；2) 修复 tenant-service 默认缺失 AssetIngestionPipeline bean 导致服务无法启动的问题；3) tenant-service 调用 model-service 改为显式 JSON + JDK HttpClient HTTP/1.1，解决 uvicorn 因 h2c upgrade 报 Unsupported upgrade request 并回落成 FastAPI 422 body missing 的问题；4) 本地通过 API 和 Playwright 验证 copy/image/video 三类任务都能成功，视频轮询可转 succeeded，钱包从 1925.9890 扣减到 1902.9890；5) 当前干净 demo 数据默认只放开 copy-standard，联调时已通过平台超管 API 为 tenant-demo 临时补充 image-standard 与 video-standard 访问规则，若重置数据库需要重新补齐或把该 seed 固化到代码里。

features.title: Implement audit log query API and admin frontend page
features.description: Audit events are written but not queryable. Add a paginated query endpoint on the platform service and a corresponding list page in the admin frontend.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/admin/audit-events supports filtering by tenant, time range, and event type
Response is paginated
Admin frontend shows audit log list with filters

features.title: Implement multimodal asset upload with ES vector storage
features.description: The asset library supports listing but not uploading. Add a multipart file upload endpoint on the tenant service. After upload, Python model gateway parses the asset (PDF/Word→text chunks, image→CLIP description, video→keyframe description), generates embeddings, and stores chunks in Elasticsearch vector index (asset_chunks) for semantic retrieval during brand grounding. Asset metadata is persisted in PostgreSQL as before.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/tenant/assets/upload accepts image, video, and document files
Uploaded files are stored on local disk and asset metadata is persisted in PostgreSQL
Python parsing pipeline chunks documents and generates embeddings
Chunks are indexed in Elasticsearch with tenant_id, brand_id, asset_id filters
Brand grounding can retrieve relevant chunks by semantic similarity
Frontend brand asset page shows an upload button and reflects new assets after upload

features.title: Complete tenant recharge frontend flow
features.description: The recharge API exists but the frontend recharge page is incomplete. Implement the full recharge UI: amount input, order creation, QR display, payment status polling, and wallet balance refresh.
acceptance_criteria: Tenant user can enter an amount and create a WeChat payment order from the frontend
Frontend displays mock QR code and polls for payment status
Wallet balance updates automatically after mock payment success
Wallet ledger list is visible in the frontend

features.title: Complete frontend creation workbench
features.description: The creation workbench in the frontend is a skeleton. Implement the full generation UI for copy, image, and video modalities, wired to the model gateway API.
acceptance_criteria: User can select brand, enter prompt, and submit a copy generation request
User can select brand, enter description, and submit an image generation request
User can submit a video generation request and poll for async result
Generation history list shows past results

session_notes.note: Current workbench is functionally wired to tenant generation APIs, but still needs a dedicated UI polish pass after end-to-end browser verification. Also keep an eye on login-form state behavior during automated browser testing so account switching does not carry stale password text.
